title: Hitachi Reverse Mentoring DEI Program (2024)
text: Back to Projects

title: Hitachi Reverse Mentoring DEI Program (2024)
text:
Project Overview
The Hitachi Reverse Mentoring DEI Program was a client-facing consulting project focused on designing a structured multi-generational collaboration initiative for Hitachi. The project explored how reverse mentoring could strengthen communication, improve understanding across generations, and support a more inclusive and connected workplace environment.
Rather than launching the program directly, the team developed a practical foundation that Hitachi could use for future implementation. The project combined workplace research, program planning, communication materials, and implementation-oriented content into a unified deliverable designed for real organizational use.

title: Hitachi Reverse Mentoring DEI Program (2024)
text:
Key Achievements

Contributed to the design of a structured reverse mentoring and multi-generational collaboration program
Supported research on workplace communication across generations and reverse mentoring practices
Helped develop client-ready materials that could support future internal rollout
Designed infographic materials tailored to different generational audiences
Supported the organization of program content into a clear and implementation-oriented final deliverable
Helped translate DEI goals into a more practical and workplace-focused program framework

title: Hitachi Reverse Mentoring DEI Program (2024)
text:
My Contribution
I contributed to the development of the Hitachi Reverse Mentoring DEI Program by supporting the design of a workplace initiative aimed at improving communication and collaboration across generations.
My work focused on helping shape research-backed content and communication materials that could turn a broad DEI concept into a more structured and actionable program. In particular, I designed infographic materials tailored to different generational audiences, helping communicate the purpose, structure, and value of the proposed program in a more accessible and visually engaging way.

Research and Program Development

Supported research on reverse mentoring practices and multi-generational workplace communication
Helped organize insights on how employees from different generations could learn from one another through structured interaction
Contributed to the development of a program concept that connected employee engagement with communication improvement
Assisted in shaping supporting content that strengthened the rationale behind the proposed initiative



Communication and Content Support

Designed infographic materials for different generational audiences to introduce and promote the program
Created visual content that communicated the program’s purpose, structure, and value in a clear and engaging way
Helped translate research findings into more accessible and audience-friendly messaging
Contributed to communication materials that supported internal awareness and future program rollout



Implementation-Oriented Project Support

Helped develop supporting materials for participant understanding and future program rollout
Contributed to content such as mindset readings, communication assets, and structured program guidance
Assisted in organizing the final project output into a professional and client-ready format
Supported the creation of a deliverable that was both research-informed and practical for business use

title: Hitachi Reverse Mentoring DEI Program (2024)
text:
Infographic Preview




Infographic I designed for the older generation audience to introduce the program and encourage participation.

title: Hitachi Reverse Mentoring DEI Program (2024)
text:
Business Impact
This project supported Hitachi in exploring a more structured approach to multi-generational communication, employee engagement, and inclusive workplace learning.
By developing research-backed program materials and communication assets, the project provided the client with a clearer path for future implementation. Instead of presenting reverse mentoring only as a concept, the team created a more practical framework that could help the organization communicate, support, and potentially launch the initiative internally.
The project also demonstrated how DEI-focused ideas can become more actionable when supported by research, structured planning, and clear communication design.

title: Hitachi Reverse Mentoring DEI Program (2024)
text:
Project Execution & Learning
This experience strengthened my ability to contribute to projects that connect workplace communication, organizational strategy, and structured program design.
Through this project, I gained hands-on experience in turning broad business and DEI objectives into practical deliverables that could support real implementation. I also developed a stronger understanding of how generational differences can influence communication and collaboration, and how thoughtful program design can help bridge those gaps in a professional environment.
The project strengthened my skills in research synthesis, content development, visual communication, and team-based project execution. It also gave me experience contributing to a client-oriented initiative where the final deliverable needed to be both well-supported and realistically usable.

title: AiPiann Student Talent Database (2026)

title: AiPiann Student Talent Database (2026)
text:
Project Overview
AiPiann Student Talent Database is a web-based platform designed to connect students and employers through a structured talent discovery system. The platform enables students to present their skills, projects, and career interests, while allowing companies to search for suitable candidates through filters and AI-supported matching.
The project combines profile data collection, dashboard functionality, company-side search features, API integration, and profile enrichment workflows into a unified system. Its development emphasized usability, testing discipline, and product reliability across both user-facing and technical components.

title: AiPiann Student Talent Database (2026)
text:
Key Achievements

Led quality assurance efforts across major platform workflows
Primarily supported the development and validation of company-side features
Assisted both frontend and backend development through testing and feature validation
Designed structured test plans and test cases to improve feature reliability
Strengthened release readiness through regression testing, issue tracking, and workflow validation
Helped improve search behavior, interface consistency, and overall platform usability

title: AiPiann Student Talent Database (2026)
text:
My Contribution
I served as the QA Lead for the project and primarily supported the development of the company-side features of the platform, while also assisting with both frontend and backend development.
My work focused on improving feature reliability, validating system behavior, and supporting the development of employer-facing workflows. In addition to leading quality assurance efforts, I collaborated with teammates across frontend and backend tasks to help identify issues, verify expected functionality, and improve the overall user experience of the platform.

QA Leadership

Developed structured test plans and feature-based test cases for core platform workflows
Documented bugs with clear reproduction steps, severity levels, and validation results
Conducted regression testing to ensure fixes and updates maintained expected functionality
Performed cross-browser and mobile testing to improve consistency across devices
Supported user acceptance testing and release-readiness validation



Company-Side Development Support

Primarily supported the development and validation of employer-facing platform features
Assisted in testing company search functionality, filtering behavior, and related workflows
Helped identify logic, usability, and interaction issues affecting the company-side experience
Verified that company-side workflows aligned with expected functional behavior



Frontend and Backend Support

Assisted with frontend and backend feature validation during development
Helped test system behavior across connected components and user flows
Supported troubleshooting efforts by identifying issues across implementation logic and feature interaction
Collaborated with teammates to improve reliability across both user-facing and technical features

title: AiPiann Student Talent Database (2026)

Business Impact
This project contributed to the development of a more reliable and user-oriented platform for student talent discovery.
Through structured testing and issue validation, my work supported the team in improving the quality of critical workflows such as profile submission, dashboard interaction, search functionality, and feature updates. These efforts helped reduce risk before deployment and improved confidence in the platform’s usability and functional stability.
The project also reflected broader goals around documentation, testing coverage, system performance, and upgrade reliability, all of which were identified as key success standards in the project plan.

title: AiPiann Student Talent Database (2026)
text:
Project Execution & Learning
This experience strengthened my ability to contribute at the intersection of product quality and software development.
By leading testing efforts while also supporting frontend and backend workflows, I developed a stronger understanding of how reliable products are built through both implementation and validation. I also improved my ability to communicate technical issues clearly, organize testing processes, and collaborate within a team environment where functionality, usability, and deployment readiness all had to align.
The project gave me practical experience in quality assurance, issue tracking, cross-functional collaboration, and end-to-end feature validation within a multi-component web platform.

title: Home
text:
Hello! Welcome to my personal profile! I’m a graduate student in Business Analytics and AI at The University of Texas at Dallas. I am interested in data analytics, operations, and supply chain analytics, and I enjoy using data to support better decision-making and improve business performance.


Through my academic, internship, and project experience, I have built hands-on skills in data analysis, reporting, pricing analytics, risk-related data work, and cross-functional collaboration. I am currently involved in an industry-sponsored project with FleetPride, where I help develop a pricing analytics framework and multi-level price elasticity models to evaluate how pricing decisions affect demand, revenue, and gross profit.

title: A/B Testing Case Studies & Experimental Insights (2025)
text: Back to Projects

title: A/B Testing Case Studies & Experimental Insights (2025)
text:
Project Overview
This project focuses on analyzing a series of A/B testing and experimental design case studies across digital platforms, marketplace operations, and behavioral policy interventions. The objective was to understand how organizations use experimentation to evaluate product changes, measure treatment effects, and make more informed business decisions.
By examining real-world cases from companies and public-sector organizations, the project translated experimental concepts into practical analytical insights. It emphasized not only whether an intervention was statistically effective, but also whether the results were meaningful enough to justify implementation at scale.

title: A/B Testing Case Studies & Experimental Insights (2025)
text:
Key Achievements

Analyzed multiple real-world A/B testing and field experiment cases across product, platform, and policy settings.

Evaluated treatment effects using experimental design logic, hypothesis testing, and confidence interval interpretation.

Assessed both statistical significance and business significance when interpreting experiment results.

Identified potential bias, implementation risk, and trade-offs in experiment design and rollout decisions.

Connected case evidence to clear managerial recommendations supported by data and experimental reasoning.

Strengthened understanding of how experimentation supports decision-making in dynamic business environments.

title: A/B Testing Case Studies & Experimental Insights (2025)
section: My Contribution: Case Analysis & Experimental Interpretation
text:
My Contribution: Case Analysis & Experimental Interpretation
While the overall project focused on case-based learning in experimentation and causal inference, my primary responsibility was organizing the analytical structure behind each case and translating experimental results into clear business insights.
The case study analysis involved:

Defining treatment, control, and primary outcome metrics

Interpreting observed treatment effects and their practical implications

Evaluating statistical significance and confidence intervals

Identifying opportunity cost, risk, and design limitations

Formulating evidence-based recommendations for rollout or further testing


Case Study Coverage
The project included experimental analysis across several real-world cases, including:

A/B Testing at Vungle: evaluating the business impact of an updated ad-serving strategy

Behavioural Insights Team (A): examining the use of behavioral nudges in public policy communication

Behavior Change for Good: understanding large-scale intervention design and experimentation strategy

Uber Case: analyzing experiment trade-offs in platform operations and marketplace performance

title: A/B Testing Case Studies & Experimental Insights (2025)
text:
Business Impact
These case studies illustrate how experimentation creates business value by:

Reducing decision-making based solely on intuition

Quantifying the effect of product, policy, or operational changes

Supporting evidence-based rollout decisions

Revealing trade-offs between user experience, efficiency, and revenue outcomes

Improving confidence in strategic recommendations through structured testing

title: A/B Testing Case Studies & Experimental Insights (2025)
text:
Project Execution & Learning
This project required more than simply summarizing case materials. It involved breaking down each experiment into its core components, evaluating whether the research design supported causal conclusions, and determining whether the results were strong enough to support real-world action.
By studying cases across different industries and contexts, I gained a stronger understanding of how experimental thinking is applied beyond the classroom, especially when business decisions involve uncertainty, partial evidence, and implementation cost.
This experience strengthened my ability to:

Break down complex experiments into clear analytical components

Distinguish statistical significance from practical business impact

Recognize limitations in experimental design and external validity

Communicate experimental findings in a structured and decision-oriented way

title: A/B Testing Case Studies & Experimental Insights (2025)
text:
Skills & Tools
A/B Testing | Causal Inference | Statistical Analysis | Experimental Design | Hypothesis Testing | Confidence Intervals | R
Back to Projects

title: Spreadsheet Modeling for Business Decision Analysis (2025)
text:
Excel-based coursework in optimization, simulation, and decision analysis
Back to Projects

title: Spreadsheet Modeling for Business Decision Analysis (2025)
text:
Project Overview
This coursework focused on applying spreadsheet modeling techniques in Excel to solve business decision problems related to pricing, optimization, uncertainty, and sequential decision-making. Through a combination of modeling exercises and exam-based applications, I built structured spreadsheet models to evaluate trade-offs and support analytical decision-making.
The coursework emphasized turning business scenarios into organized frameworks by defining inputs, decision variables, objective functions, and constraints. It also strengthened my ability to use Excel as an analytical and decision-support tool, rather than only for basic calculations.

title: Spreadsheet Modeling for Business Decision Analysis (2025)
text:
Technical Methodology
I used Microsoft Excel to build and test spreadsheet-based decision models across several business contexts. My work involved using formulas, lookup functions, and optimization tools to organize data, compare alternatives, and interpret results.
The methods I applied included What-If Analysis, Goal Seek, Data Tables, XLOOKUP, PivotTables, and Excel Solver. In my modeling work, XLOOKUP was used to connect calculated outputs with their corresponding decision values, while PivotTables were used in supporting coursework exercises to summarize and explore dataset patterns across categories and time periods.

title: Spreadsheet Modeling for Business Decision Analysis (2025)
text:
Key Learning Outcomes
Through this coursework, I learned how to structure business problems into clear analytical models and evaluate decisions more systematically. I strengthened my ability to connect spreadsheet logic with practical business questions.
Some of the key skills I developed include:

Building pricing and profit models based on cost, demand, and revenue assumptions

Using Excel Solver to optimize decisions under defined constraints

Applying XLOOKUP to return relevant input or decision values from model results

Using PivotTables to summarize and review supporting data efficiently

Evaluating alternatives through scenario analysis, simulation, and decision tree logic

Interpreting outputs and translating them into clearer business recommendations


Excel Examples

What-If Analysis for Pricing Optimization


This what-if analysis table compares multiple price combinations to identify the maximum total profit outcome. The highlighted cell shows the best result within the tested range and demonstrates how Excel can be used to support structured pricing analysis.



Solver-Based Optimization Model


This Solver-based optimization model identifies the most cost-efficient shipment plan from each preparation center to each store. The final solution minimizes total transportation and preparation cost to $1,565, while meeting all store demand requirements and respecting processing capacity constraints at each center.



XLOOKUP for Data Enrichment




This example shows how I used XLOOKUP to retrieve the corresponding median income for each income level from a cleaned reference table. I then divided that value by family size to create an income-per-member measure, allowing income to be compared more consistently across households of different sizes.



PivotTable for Income Pattern Analysis


This PivotTable summarizes the average income per household member by combinations of mother’s and father’s education levels. The pattern suggests that higher parental education is generally associated with higher household income, with a stronger upward trend appearing across the mother’s education levels.

title: Spreadsheet Modeling for Business Decision Analysis (2025)
text:
Business Value
This coursework demonstrates how spreadsheet modeling can support more structured and informed decision-making. It shows my ability to organize complex business problems, compare alternatives, and evaluate trade-offs between cost, revenue, risk, and operational constraints.
More importantly, it helped me understand how spreadsheet models can bridge quantitative analysis and managerial decision-making in a practical business setting.

title: Spreadsheet Modeling for Business Decision Analysis (2025)
text:

Platform
Microsoft Excel
Spreadsheet Techniques
Formula Modeling, XLOOKUP, PivotTable, What-If Analysis, Goal Seek, Data Tables
Optimization Tools

Analytics Techniques

title: About Me
text:
Hi, I’m Sharon. I am originally from Taipei, Taiwan. From a young age, I have always enjoyed exploring different interests and experiences. Growing up, I participated in dance, choir, drama, and piano, and one of my favorite childhood memories was performing with my school choir at Taipei Arena, a major indoor venue in Taipei for concerts and public events.
In 2018, during my second year of high school, I moved to Austin, Texas, and began a new chapter of my life in the United States. As I adjusted to a new environment, I volunteered at an animal shelter with friends, and one of the most meaningful parts of that experience was seeing the animals I cared for find new homes. In 2019, I also received a Regional Medal at the Texas VASE Regional Competition, which reflected my continued love for art and creativity.





 


Volunteer - Austin Humane Society (2019)











Texas VASE Regional Competition (2019)


Outside of school and work, I enjoy drawing, photography, cooking, singing, and watching anime. These interests continue to shape how I see the world and how I express myself.


 







 







 


Some Photography



My college years brought many meaningful experiences, especially after I transferred to The University of Texas at Dallas. During my time at UTD, I grew through volunteering, creative projects, academic achievements, and hands-on experiences that eventually led me to continue into the MSBA program.






Volunteer - Feed My Starving Children (2023)




 


Volunteer - AIESEC Summer Camp (2023)



This website is a space where I share both my professional journey and the experiences that have shaped who I am. I’m glad you’re here, and I hope this page helps you get to know me a little better. 😊

title: Pricing Analytics Dashboard & Elasticity Insights (2026)
text: Back to Projects

title: Pricing Analytics Dashboard & Elasticity Insights (2026)
text:
Project Overview
FleetPride is one of the largest distributors of aftermarket heavy-duty truck and equipment parts in North America. This project focuses on developing a pricing analytics and price-elasticity framework to help the company better understand how pricing decisions affect demand, revenue, and profitability.
The goal is to translate complex transaction-level data and modeling outputs into actionable insights that support pricing strategy and business decision-making. As part of this project, I developed an interactive KPI dashboard to serve as the decision-support layer.

title: Pricing Analytics Dashboard & Elasticity Insights (2026)
text:
Key Achievements

Built an interactive KPI dashboard to support pricing decisions by visualizing revenue, profit, margin, and sales performance across products and customers.

Translated complex pricing and elasticity concepts into intuitive visual tools for business users.
Designed target benchmarking using rolling 3-month averages to evaluate performance gaps.

Enabled multi-dimensional analysis across SKU, customer, and segment levels.

Identified high-performing products and key customer groups through dynamic filtering and ranking.

Developed price elasticity visualizations to support understanding of demand sensitivity.
Integrated competitor pricing comparisons to support pricing strategy evaluation.

title: Pricing Analytics Dashboard & Elasticity Insights (2026)
text:
My Contribution: KPI Visualization Layer
While the overall project focuses on building a price elasticity modeling framework, my primary responsibility was developing the visualization and decision-support layer.
The dashboard enables users to:

Analyze revenue, profit, and margin across SKUs and customers

Track performance trends and detect growth patterns

Evaluate performance against target benchmarks

Explore customer behavior, including repeat purchases and bulk buying

Interpret pricing impact through elasticity and competitor comparisons


Dashboard Preview

title: Pricing Analytics Dashboard & Elasticity Insights (2026)
text:
Business Impact
The KPI dashboard bridges the gap between modeling and decision-making by:

Making pricing insights accessible to non-technical stakeholders

Supporting data-driven pricing and revenue optimization decisions

Highlighting underperforming products and segments

Providing visibility into customer purchasing behavior

Enabling faster and more informed pricing strategy evaluation

title: Pricing Analytics Dashboard & Elasticity Insights (2026)
text:
Project Execution & Learning
This project involved real-world constraints, including delayed sponsor data. Instead of waiting, we proactively built a working prototype using synthetic data to develop the dashboard structure and KPI logic in advance.
By designing a modular and flexible system, we were able to seamlessly integrate sponsor data once it became available without disrupting the overall project timeline.
This experience strengthened my ability to:

Work effectively under uncertainty

Maintain project momentum in real-world settings

Design scalable analytics solutions adaptable to changing data conditions
